title: Nifty VScode Tricks
description: Some vscode tricks I have learned that have helped me a ton

children.value: I have begun to move most of my colors on my website to using HSL and needed an easy way to select colors. I discovered that by clicking on the status bar in the color picker, you can change the color format. At the moment it supports RGB, Hex, and HSL.
You won't be able to use this method with colors that have an alpha value. See this
Auto format the document on save

I hate unformatted code. It's hard to read, takes long to debug, and as someone who is a bit of a perfectionist, it irks my soul. But alas, VSCode to the rescue again. Deep in the settings, you can your code formatted

props.alt: Picture of Auto-Format setting in VsCode

children.value: There's also a setting to format on paste! Great for keeping those Stack Overflow snippets formatted the same as the rest of your code.
Okay, this one isn't built into vscode, but it's an amazing plugin I discovered. As a developer
to get better at writing, this one has been useful. I'm using it right now to write this blog post.

props.alt: Picture of Write Good Linter in VsCode

children.value: and you can think of it as the Hemingway editor built into vscode. After installing it, the plugin will detect any unnecessary grammar and put a little

links.text: Auto format the document on save
